mission.title: helm: Usable sub-charts templates from parent chart
mission.description: Usable sub-charts templates from parent chart. Requested by 86+ users.

steps.title: Check current helm deployment
steps.description:
Verify your helm version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
helm list -n helm 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working helm installation.

steps.title: Review helm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
kubectl get configmap -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=helm
```
~~Templates are global are recursively namespaced~~ Named templates are global, so the guideline is to namespace them with the chart name to avoid collision.

This leads to the natural desire to use sub-charts templates from parent charts.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Usable sub-charts templates from parent chart
steps.description:
There have been many requests for a way to use templates for chart `values.yaml` (#2492, #2133, ...). The main reason being that it's currently impossible to derive the values of the subcharts from templates. However, having templates in `values.yaml` make them unparsable and create a "chicken or
```yaml
### values/subchart.yaml ###
    subchart:
        fullnameOverride: subchart-{{ .Relese.Name }}
        debug: {{ default "false" .Values.debug }}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
kubectl get events -n helm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Usable sub-charts templates from parent chart" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: There have been many requests for a way to use templates for chart `values.yaml` (#2492, #2133, ...). The main reason being that it's currently impossible to derive the values of the subcharts from templates. However, having templates in `values.yaml` make them unparsable and create a "chicken or the egg" problem when rendering them.
codeSnippets:
### Chart.yaml ###
    dependencies:
    - name: subchart
      condition: subchart.enabled
    
    ### values/subchart.yaml ###
    subchart:
    {{- if eq .Values.environment "production" -}}
      enabled: true
    {{- else -}}
      enabled: false
    {{- end -}}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
